NoteText: 62-year-old postmenopausal female (G3P3) presents with 3-month history of progressive abdominal bloating, early satiety, and unintentional 12-pound weight loss. PMH: Hypertension (amlodipine 5mg daily), Type 2 DM (metformin 1000mg BID), BMI 28.4. Family history significant for mother with breast cancer at age 58, maternal aunt with ovarian cancer at age 63. ECOG performance status 1. Pelvic ultrasound showed complex bilateral adnexal masses, right 8.2 cm and left 5.7 cm, with solid and cystic components and internal vascularity. Moderate ascites noted. CA-125 elevated at 847 U/mL (normal <35). HE4 elevated at 312 pmol/L. Referred to gynecologic oncology for further workup.

GYN Oncology consultation. Reviewed outside US and labs. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis obtained showing bilateral adnexal masses with peritoneal carcinomatosis involving the omentum, right paracolic gutter, and diaphragmatic surface. Para-aortic lymphadenopathy with largest node measuring 2.3 cm. No liver parenchymal metastases. Small bilateral pleural effusions. No pulmonary nodules. Discussed with patient: findings highly concerning for advanced epithelial ovarian cancer. Plan: 1) CT-guided biopsy of omental cake for tissue diagnosis. 2) Genetic counseling and BRCA testing. 3) Discuss at GYN tumor board for PDS vs NACT decision.
CT-guided biopsy of omental mass performed. Two cores obtained and sent to pathology. Specimen adequate. No immediate complications. Patient tolerated procedure well. Results pending.

Results reviewed with patient. Pathology confirms high-grade serous carcinoma of Mullerian origin, consistent with ovarian/fallopian tube primary. IHC: WT1+, p53 aberrant (diffuse strong overexpression), PAX8+, ER 80%+, PR 10%+, p16 block positive, CK7+, CK20-. Genetic testing results: BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC, 5382insC). Myriad myChoice HRD score: 58 (positive, cutoff 42). MMR intact (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 all intact by IHC). MSS by NGS. PD-L1 CPS 8. TMB 4.2 mut/Mb. FIGO Stage IIIC (based on para-aortic LN involvement). ECOG 1. Discussed treatment options: Given extensive peritoneal disease and omental caking, multidisciplinary recommendation is for neoadjuvant chemotherapy (NACT) x 3 cycles followed by interval debulking surgery (IDS). Recommended regimen: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q21d. Plan for PARP inhibitor maintenance given BRCA1+ status. Genetic counseling referral for family.

Cycle 1 Day 1 NACT: Carboplatin AUC 5 IV + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 IV. Pre-medications: Dexamethasone, ondansetron, diphenhydramine, famotidine. Weight 72 kg, BSA 1.78 m2. Creatinine clearance 82 mL/min. CBC within normal limits. No infusion reactions. Tolerated well. RTC in 3 weeks for cycle 2.

After 3 cycles NACT. CA-125 decreased from 847 to 89 U/mL (89% reduction). CT re-staging shows significant partial response: bilateral adnexal masses decreased (right 3.1 cm from 8.2, left 2.8 cm from 5.7). Omental disease markedly improved. Para-aortic lymphadenopathy decreased to 1.1 cm. Ascites resolved. No new disease. Assessment: Excellent response to NACT. Patient is now a candidate for interval debulking surgery. Plan: Proceed with IDS including TAH/BSO, omentectomy, pelvic and para-aortic lymph node dissection, and maximal cytoreduction. Surgery scheduled for 2025-12-08.

Interval debulking surgery performed. Procedure: Exploratory laparotomy, total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, infracolic omentectomy, bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection, para-aortic lymph node dissection up to renal vessels, appendectomy, peritoneal biopsies from bilateral diaphragm, paracolic gutters, cul-de-sac, and bladder peritoneum. Findings: Residual tumor on right ovary (2.5 cm), left fallopian tube fimbrial mass (1.8 cm), omental nodule (0.5 cm), no gross disease on peritoneal surfaces after resection. All visible disease resected. Residual disease: R0 (no gross residual). EBL 450 mL. No intraoperative complications. Frozen section confirmed HGSC in right ovary and left tube.
Final surgical pathology - see pathology_reports.csv for full synoptic report.

Post-op visit, 4 weeks after IDS. Incision healed well. CA-125 decreased to 24 U/mL. ECOG 1. Plan: Resume adjuvant chemotherapy - Carboplatin AUC 5 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 x 3 additional cycles (cycles 4-6 of total 6). After completing chemotherapy, plan to initiate olaparib 300mg BID maintenance given BRCA1+ and R0 resection (per SOLO1 trial).

Completed 6 cycles total carboplatin/paclitaxel (3 NACT + 3 adjuvant). CA-125 normalized to 12 U/mL. CT scan shows complete radiologic response - no measurable disease. Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy in fingers and toes (manageable). Plan: Initiate olaparib 300mg BID maintenance therapy. Baseline CBC, CMP, and LFTs normal. Counsel patient on side effects: nausea, fatigue, anemia. Monitor CBC monthly x 3 months, then every 3 months. CA-125 every 3 months. CT every 6 months.
